```

‚ēö‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚ēĒ‚ēĚ‚ēö‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚ēĒ‚ēĚ‚ēö‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚ēĒ‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚ēĒ‚ēĚ‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚ēó‚Ėą‚Ėą‚ēĎ ‚ēö‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚ēĎ
 ‚ēö‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēĚ  ‚ēö‚ēź‚ēź‚ĖÄ‚ĖÄ‚ēź‚ēĚ  ‚ēö‚ēź‚ēź‚ēĚ‚ēö‚ēź‚ēź‚ēĚ ‚ēö‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēĚ‚ēö‚ēź‚ēĚ  ‚ēö‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēĚ
```

**Qwen OAuth account manager + OpenAI-compatible proxy**  
No certificates. No MITM. Just accounts.

---

## Install

```bash
bunx gqwen-auth setup
```

Or install globally:

```bash
bun install -g gqwen-auth
gqwen setup
```

> Requires [Bun](https://bun.sh) ‚Č• 1.0

---

## Quick start

```bash
# 1. Add a Qwen account (opens browser for OAuth)
gqwen add

# 2. Start the proxy in the background
gqwen serve on

# 3. Done ‚ÄĒ proxy is running at http://localhost:3099
```

Use it with any OpenAI-compatible client:

```javascript
import OpenAI from 'openai'

const client = new OpenAI({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:3099/v1',
  apiKey: 'any-value',
})

const res = await client.chat.completions.create({
  model: 'qwen3-coder-plus',
  messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'Hello!' }],
})
```

Or with `curl`:

```bash
curl http://localhost:3099/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model":"qwen3-coder-plus","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Hi"}]}'
```

---

## Commands

### Setup & accounts

```bash
gqwen setup              # Interactive wizard: add ‚Üí test ‚Üí start proxy
gqwen add                # Add a Qwen account via OAuth (opens browser)
gqwen list               # List all accounts with status
gqwen remove <id>        # Remove an account by ID or email
gqwen enable <id>        # Enable a disabled account
gqwen disable <id>       # Disable an account (kept in DB, skipped in rotation)
gqwen test [id]          # Test connectivity for all accounts or one
```

### Proxy server

```bash
gqwen serve on           # Start proxy in background (daemon)
gqwen serve off          # Stop the background proxy
gqwen serve              # Show proxy status
gqwen serve logs         # Tail proxy logs in real time
gqwen serve fg           # Start in foreground (blocks terminal)
```

### Monitoring

```bash
gqwen status             # Usage stats, token counts, estimated cost, locks
gqwen models             # List models available from the Qwen API
gqwen unlock [id]        # Clear rate-limit locks for one or all accounts
```

### Configuration

```bash
gqwen config                            # Show current settings
gqwen config --strategy round-robin     # fill-first (default) | round-robin
gqwen config --port 3099                # Change proxy port
gqwen config --sticky-limit 5          # Consecutive-use limit for round-robin
```

---

## Available models

| Model | Description |
|---|---|
| `qwen3-coder-plus` | Most capable coding model |
| `qwen3-coder-flash` | Faster, lighter coding model |
| `vision-model` | Multimodal (vision + text) |
| `coder-model` | General code model |

---

## How it works

- **OAuth device flow** ‚ÄĒ logs in with your real Qwen account via browser, no passwords stored
- **OpenAI-compatible API** ‚ÄĒ drop-in replacement for `api.openai.com`, works with any existing client
- **Account rotation** ‚ÄĒ distributes requests across multiple accounts to avoid rate limits
- **Background daemon** ‚ÄĒ proxy runs detached, persists across terminal sessions
- **Token tracking** ‚ÄĒ counts prompt/completion tokens per request and estimates equivalent API cost
- **Auto token refresh** ‚ÄĒ renews expired OAuth tokens automatically

Data is stored locally in `~/.gqwen/gqwen.db` (SQLite).

---

## Uninstall

```bash
bun uninstall -g gqwen-auth

# Remove stored accounts and data (optional)
rm -rf ~/.gqwen
```
